title: A Triolet.
content:
To commemorate the virtue of Homoeopathy in restoring one apparently drowned.

     Love, that in a tear was drown'd,
     Lives revived by a tear.
     Stella heard them mourn around
     Love that in a tear was drown'd,
     Came and coax'd his dripping swound,
     Wept 'The fault was mine, my dear!'
     Love, that in a tear was drown'd,
     Lives, revived by a tear.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Arthur Thomas Quiller-Couch, titled "A Triolet.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
